II. Project Management Plan
1. Overview
1.1 Cost & Time Estimations
The Inventory and Sales Management Solution for SMEs project is conducted over a 15-week academic semester. The project effort is estimated in person-days (pds), where one person-day is equivalent to eight working hours.

Effort assumptions:
* Project duration: 15 weeks
* Working days per week: 5
* Total working days per member: 75
* Effective project allocation per member: approximately 80%
* Effective effort per member: 60 person-days
* Team size: 4 members
Total project capacity: 300 person-days

#
	Work Package
	Est. Effort (pds)
	Deadline

	Stage 1: Project Initiating (week 1)

1.3 Project Risks

No.
	Risk Description
	Impact
	Probability
	Mitigation Plan
1
	Team member workload imbalance
	High
	Medium
	Redistribute tasks and increase peer support.
2
	Limited experience with AI technologies
	High
	Medium
	Allocate time for self-study and prototyping.
3
	Requirement changes
	Medium
	High
	Freeze core requirements before implementation.
4
	Communication issues
	Medium
	Low
	Conduct regular meetings and maintain documentation.
5
	Scope expansion
	High
	Medium
	Enforce scope control and change approval process.

2. Management Approach
2.1 Project Processes

The Inventory and Sales Management Solution for SMEs project will be developed using the Agile Scrum methodology to ensure flexibility, adaptability to changes, and continuous improvement throughout the development lifecycle. The project will be divided into multiple sprints, each lasting two weeks. During each sprint, the team will prioritize and implement selected features, conduct daily stand-up meetings to update progress and resolve blockers, and perform a sprint review at the end of each iteration to demonstrate completed functionalities to stakeholders.

* Scope and Requirement Management: The team will collect and analyze requirements such as "export 50 units of Product A," "import 30 units of Product B," or "check current stock levels." Core requirements will remain fixed, while additional or extended features will be managed through a formal change request process.
* Change Management: Any proposed changes will be documented and evaluated in terms of impact on schedule, scope, and resources before approval. This approach ensures controlled adaptation without jeopardizing project timelines.
* Time Management: Task scheduling and monitoring will be supported by Scrum boards (Trello/Jira) or Excel. Work allocation will be divided as follows:
   * Backend (.NET Core): Develop RESTful APIs for warehouse operations such as import, export, and inventory management, and integrate with SQL Server/MySQL.
   * Voice Recognition Module: Integrate speech-to-text APIs (e.g., Google Speech-to-Text or VOSK) to convert voice commands into SQL statements.
   * Frontend (Next.js): Build an intuitive web interface to process import/export requests, display inventory reports, and visualize results retrieved from the backend.
   * Database: Design schemas for products, stock-in, stock-out, and inventory tracking.
* Supporting Processes:
   * Version Control: Managed with Git and GitHub, using branching strategies (main, dev, and feature branches).
   * Risk Management: Identifying potential risks such as inaccurate voice recognition results; fallback solutions (manual input) will be prepared.
   * Communication Management: Daily communication through Zalo/Discord and weekly online meetings via Google Meet.

2.2 Quality Management
Defect Prevention: Development will follow standard coding conventions for both C# (.NET) and JavaScript (Next.js). Code commits will be checked against linters (ESLint for Next.js, StyleCop or equivalent for C#). A peer review process will be applied before merging into the main branch to minimize defects early in the development cycle.

Reviewing:
* Peer Reviews: Cross-checking of source code among team members prior to merging.
* Sprint Reviews: Evaluation of completed functionalities at the end of each sprint with feedback from stakeholders.

Testing: Multiple levels of testing will be conducted to ensure system reliability:
* Unit Testing: Focused on individual functions/modules such as business logic in the .NET backend (e.g., stock-in/stock-out processing).
* Integration Testing: Validating interactions between backend APIs, the database, and the Next.js frontend.
* System Testing: End-to-end validation of warehouse workflows, including both voice and manual operations.
* User Acceptance Testing (UAT): Conducted with instructors or simulated end-users to assess usability, accuracy, and completeness of features.

Quality Metrics: The following measurable goals will be applied:
* Defect rate per sprint kept below 5%.
* At least 90% of initial requirements fully implemented.
* Voice-to-SQL accuracy of ≥ 80%, with fallback options for manual editing.
* Frontend designed for usability: warehouse operations should require no more than 3 steps.

2.3 Project Training Plan
Training Area
	Participants
	When, Duration
.NET Core Backend Development (REST APIs, business logic, SQL Server/MySQL integration)
	Backend developers
	Weeks 1–2, 3 sessions (2 hours each)
Next.js Frontend Development (building web interfaces, handling API requests, rendering reports)
	Frontend developers
	Weeks 1–2, 3 sessions (2 hours each)
SQL & Database Design (schema design for warehouse management, writing optimized queries)
	Backend developers + 1–2 frontend members
	Week 2, 2 sessions (2 hours each)
Git & GitHub (branching strategy, pull requests, code review workflow)
	All team members
	Week 1, 2 sessions (1.5 hours each)
Voice Recognition APIs (Google Speech-to-Text, VOSK integration, converting text to SQL)
	AI/voice module developers
	Weeks 2–3, 2 sessions (2 hours each)
Agile/Scrum Workflow (backlog management, sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint review)
	Project manager + all team members
	Before Sprint 1, 1 session (1.5 hours)

4. Project Communications

Communication Item
	Who/ Target
	Purpose
	When, Frequency
	Type, Tool, Method(s)
Daily Meeting
	Team
	Share progress, discuss problem, align task
	21:00 every day (15-30 minutes)
	Google Meet
Weekly Meeting
	Team, Instructor
	Review project progress, confirm plan
	11:00 Monday (1 hour)
	Face-to-face meeting
Issue Solution
	Team, Instructor
	Report and find way to resolve issue
	As needed, immediate if critical
	Email, Google Meet, Direct call (urgent)
Demo
	Team, Instructor
	Demonstrate complete features, collect feedback
	Every 2 weeks (end of each implementation iteration)
	Google Meet, face-to-face meeting
User feedback collection
	Warehouse staff, Team
	Gather information such as usability and user friendliness
	During User Acceptance Testing
	Google form, feedback session
Training session
	Warehouse staff
	Ensure staff can use system effectively before launch
	1-2 weeks before going public
	Record video, user manual

5. Configuration Management
Configuration Management is applied in the Inventory and Sales Management Solution for SMEs (ISMS) project to ensure that all project artifacts, including documents, source code, and system configurations, are properly controlled, tracked, and maintained throughout the project lifecycle.

5.1 Document Management
All project documents (requirements, design documents, reports, test plans, and final deliverables) of the Inventory and Sales Management Solution for SMEs will be stored centrally in the team's Google Drive.
* Versioning: Each document follows a clear version naming convention such as v0.9, v1.0, and v1.1 to reflect its maturity level.
* Change Control: When a document is updated, the responsible member must record the change in the Document Change Log and notify the team to ensure everyone is working with the latest version.
* Backups: Weekly backups are performed for all important reports and documents to prevent data loss.

5.2 Source Code Management
The team uses GitHub as the central source code repository for the Inventory and Sales Management Solution for SMEs (ISMS).
* Branching Model: Git Flow is applied with the following branches: main, develop, feature/*, and bugfix/*.
* Commit Convention: Code commits follow a standard naming convention, for example:
   * feat: add inventory adjustment API
   * fix: correct stock calculation logic
* Code Review: All code changes must be reviewed before being merged into the main branch to ensure code quality and consistency.

5.3 Release Management
Software releases are managed and tracked throughout the project lifecycle.
* Each major delivery is packaged as a software release (Software Package Version 1, Version 2, Version 3).
* Source code, database scripts, and related documents are synchronized for each release.
* Only stable and tested versions are used for demonstrations and final submissions.

5.4 Tools & Infrastructures

Category
	Tools / Infrastructure
	Technology
Development Frameworks
	Next.js (Frontend), .NET Core (Backend)

	SQL Server
IDEs/Editors

	Draw.io
Documentation
	MS Office, Google Docs/Sheets/Slides

	GitHub (Source Codes), Google Drive (Documents)
Deployment Server
	Amazon Web Services
Project Management
	GitLab: Source code hub, Excel: Task & project tracking</content>
